read_this_first: This is a DRY-RUN EVIDENCE ASSESSMENT, not a reconciliation record. It classifies what evidence exists or is missing per deliverable; it does not register, bind, or authorize anything.

Metadata backfill (deliverable_role/locale/publication_destination/publication_path, applied to production 2026-07-15) is NOT artifact binding. Populating those columns tells the evaluator what a deliverable IS and WHERE it should live; it says nothing about whether the image, PDF, webpage, or external post actually exists as registered, verified evidence. Zero publication_artifacts rows exist for any of this period's 13 deliverables, confirmed live against production at the time this note was last revised (2026-07-15T20:05Z) -- the same zero as before the metadata migrations applied.

No publication receipt was invented anywhere in this file. Every 'verified_and_bindable' classification below traces to a live HTTP check the migration file's own header records (drglaw.ca, 2026-07-15); every classification that could not be checked is marked missing, inaccessible_with_current_permissions, or pending_legal_approval, never assumed passing.

The PT 'Clause in the Margin' deliverable (b767ef14-dd4e-405e-9c54-1f7f9364f13c) remains in_review with approved_version_id = null. Reconfirmed directly against production 2026-07-15T20:05Z. No migration, script, or action in this repository touches that row's status or approval.

The period (950bad0b-fef6-4c5a-b949-fef5d9cbee90) is readiness_lifecycle = 'legacy_unreconciled' in production, reconfirmed live 2026-07-15T20:05Z. It has not been activated (enforced) and this document does not activate it.

This document does NOT claim historical evidence reconciliation is complete. Reconciliation (registering real, personally-verified publication_artifacts evidence and running reconcile-artifacts against it) has not started for this period. See 'remaining_work' at the end of this file.
note: Originally generated 2026-07-15T19:31Z as a forward-looking dry run, before this branch's 11 metadata/lifecycle migrations were applied to production. Those migrations have SINCE been applied (2026-07-15, before the second Codex review -- see the PR's incident note) and this file's predicted period lifecycle and display states were reconfirmed live against production 2026-07-15T20:05Z: they match exactly (legacy_unreconciled, 13 historical_unreconciled, 1 excluded, zero publication_artifacts rows). The evidence classifications below were not regenerated after the migration applied, because nothing that would change them changed: no publication_artifacts row was registered, no approval status changed, no new HTTP checks were run. Reproduction path: (1) scripts/dry-run-fetch-relocation-clause.mjs read-only-fetches each deliverable's production row and current-version state; (2) scripts/generate-relocation-clause-dry-run-manifest.ts (run via `npx tsx scripts/generate-relocation-clause-dry-run-manifest.ts`, read-only against production) feeds that same data, plus the metadata overlay this branch's migration writes verbatim (supabase/migrations/20260715193201_20260715120500_relocation_clause_publication_metadata.sql, hardcoded in the script and cross-checked against the migration text), into the real, unmodified evaluateDeliverableReadiness/deriveDisplayState functions from src/lib/publication-readiness.ts -- its output for all 14 rows was verified to match every evaluator_output field below, byte for byte, before this file was finalized; (3) the evidence classification per deliverable below (verified_and_bindable/missing/inaccessible_with_current_permissions/pending_legal_approval/not_applicable) is a separate, hand-authored layer on top of that computed output, sourced from the same migration file's live-verification comments (HTTP checks already performed and recorded there) plus the operating-model doc's precedent for what a dry run may and may not check (e.g. never submitting a live client-facing form) -- the evaluator does not compute this classification itself, since it has no access to LinkedIn/GBP or knowledge of which 404s are deploy gaps versus never-authored content. No evidence is fabricated anywhere in this file.

generated_by.note: dry-run script, no operator session token, no production write beyond the already-applied metadata migrations this file assesses

display_summary_confirmed_live.note: Predicted by this file's original dry run, then reconfirmed by direct query against production 2026-07-15T20:05Z after the migrations applied. Matches exactly. This is a display-state prediction check, not an evidence-completeness claim -- 'historical_unreconciled' means the operator-facing UI correctly shows 'Not yet reconciled' instead of 'Blocked' for these 13 rows; it does not mean their evidence is registered or verified.

original_production_summary_before_migra.note: The misleading pre-fix state: every active deliverable read Blocked because deliverable_role/locale/publication_destination/publication_path were all null and readiness_lifecycle did not exist as a column. Superseded in production by the metadata + lifecycle migrations, 2026-07-15. Recorded here for the historical record, not as a current claim.

remaining_work.summary: Real historical evidence/placement reconciliation for this period has not started. Everything applied to production so far is metadata (what a deliverable IS and where it SHOULD live), not evidence (proof it actually IS there).
items: Register publication_artifacts rows for the 2 deliverables classified fully verified_and_bindable (the EN counsel-note webpage, the EN checklist PDF), plus the 1 partially bindable item (the EN checklist landing page's webpage route -- the route itself is verified_and_bindable, but that same deliverable's delivery_email_present and thank_you_page_present requirements remain inaccessible_with_current_permissions, not bindable) -- an operator must personally load each URL/download each file and record it via the manual insert path in docs/PUBLICATION_READINESS_OPERATING_MODEL.md, then run POST /api/admin/content-deliverables/[deliverableId]/reconcile-artifacts to validate it.

Deploy drg-law-website to close the 3 confirmed 404 deploy-gap items (demolition-clause-ontario EN article, relocation-clause-checklist PT PDF, relocation-clause-checklist PT landing page) -- these are pre-existing production gaps independent of Publication Readiness. (The PT counsel-note is a separate 'never authored' gap, not a deploy timing issue; see the next item.)

Author the PT relocation-clause and demolition-clause article pages (no PT source exists anywhere for either), or make an operator decision to drop the PT locale requirement for those two specific deliverables.
Obtain LinkedIn and Google Business Profile account access (or operator-supplied screenshots/permalinks) to check the 5 deliverables currently classified inaccessible_with_current_permissions.
Await the firm's lawyer's review of the PT 'Clause in the Margin' deliverable (b767ef14), currently in_review -- no reconciliation action can substitute for that approval.
Register campaign/hero images for every article, GBP post, and LinkedIn post in this period (zero image evidence exists for any of the 13 deliverables today).

Only after all of the above: consider whether to activate (enforce) this period via POST /api/portal/[firmId]/periods/[periodId]/activate-readiness. Activation is a separate, later, operator-only decision this document does not make and does not recommend a timeline for.
